c74object.name: tap.808.rim~
c74object.digest: TR-808-style rimshot/claves voice, synthesized from the analog circuit
c74object.description: The tap.808.rim~ object is a circuit-informed model of the Roland TR-808's RS/CL channel — one circuit the hardware switches between two sounds. The rimshot is the ~1667 and ~455 Hz resonator pair cut to a ~10 ms crack by the channel's harmonic-generating VCA; the claves model is a single pure ~2500 Hz woody tick, ~25 ms. Trigger with a signal rising edge (amplitude 0..1 = accent) or bang / trigger messages. This channel has no noise source, so renders are fully deterministic.

inlet.digest: Trigger: a rising edge fires the voice; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent

method.name: signal
method.description: A rising edge triggers the voice, sample-accurately; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent.

method.name: bang
method.description: Trigger at full accent (from the scheduler).

method.name: trigger
method.description: Trigger with an accent value (0..1, default 1).
